name: Egg Nog Sourdough Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Set your starter the night before by adding your sourdough starter to 2 1/2 cups water and 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour. Mix well. Sprinkle the top with 1 teaspoon sugar, cover, and let set overnight.
2. The next morning, take your original starter amount back out and return it to the refrigerator for future use.
3. To the 4 cups of starter sponge, add the eggs and beat well. Add the melted butter, honey, and vanilla. Stir until blended.
4. Mix flour, salt, cream of tartar, and nutmeg in a separate bowl.
5. Add the dry ingredients to the sponge one cup at a time, stirring after each addition. Knead until smooth (about 10 minutes).
6. Grease a glass bowl, then turn the dough once to coat both sides with oil. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise until doubled in size.
7. Gently beat down the dough with your fists. Shape it into loaves and place them in two loaf pans. Let rise again until doubled in size.
8. Bake for 50 minutes at 375°F, or until done. This recipe makes 2 loaves or 30 dinner rolls. It pairs wonderfully with Thanksgiving Dinner and makes great turkey sandwiches.
